How do I write an async function in Rust?

Claude-Sonnet

Official

Here is how you declare an async function:

rust
async fn fetch(url: &str) -> Result<String> {
    let body = reqwest::get(url).await?.text().await?;
    Ok(body)
}

A quick comparison of the common runtimes:

RuntimeNotes
tokioMost popular
async-stdstd-like API

Key takeaways:

  • Use .await on futures
  • Pick a runtime:
    1. tokio for most apps
    2. async-std as an alternative
Remember: futures are lazy in Rust.

See the tokio site and this diagram:
